text: Delorme Operation A Video Technique. We used this approach for the treatment of full thickness rectal prolapse in twenty years old female patient. With no prior history of vaginal delivery. The patient had a Wexner fecal incontinence score of ten. Due to patient's fertility fear, she refused ventral mesh rectopexy.

Patient received polyethylene glycol enema on the day of operation. One gram second generation cephalosporin and five hundred milligrams metronidazole at the time of the procedure. The procedure was performed with the patient in modified lithotomy position under spinal anesthesia.

The surgeon injects adrenalized saline circumferentially into the submucosa just above the dentate line to facilitate dissection. Mucosal stripping is initiated using electrocautery, starting one centimeter away from the dentate line and extending towards the prolapse apex.

The surgeon vertically plicates the underlying muscle in four quadrants using Vicryl 3-0 sutures, starting from the apex of the dissection down to the distal cut edge of the mucosa. The sutures are placed, tagged, and additional sutures are added in between, totaling eight to ten sutures, effectively plicating the muscle.
The surgeon excises the excess mucosa and performs interrupted mucosa-to-mucosa suturing using Vicryl-III-O sutures, ensuring precise alignment of the mucosal edges.

The surgeon performs an augmented post-anal repair through a five-centimeter curvilinear incision, dissecting into the intersphincteric space up to the upper part of the external anal sphincter and puborectalis muscle. The levator ani muscle and mesorectal fat are exposed, followed by the approximation of the levator ani using Prolene 2-0 sutures.
The surgeon completes the procedure with skin closure of the surgical site.
